steps.title: Use Banerjee's contrast
steps.content: Banerjee argues that character does not need to control behavior perfectly to be meaningful. We expect a helpful person to be frequently helpful, not helpful in every possible situation regardless of circumstances.

steps.title: Reject the wrong standards
steps.content: Choice A concerns selfish motivation, not consistency. Choice B means only occasional, which conflicts with the stated expectation of frequent helpfulness. Choice C concerns reluctance, not how consistently character predicts behavior. The answer is D.
